jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 2+ years of non-internship design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience programming with at least one software programming language
jobs.description:
Alexa+ is Amazon's next-generation personal assistant powered by generative AI – more conversational, smarter, personalized, and built to get things done on any device.

The Alexa 3P Experiences team is on a mission to expand what Alexa+ can do for customers by building consumer-facing experiences with trusted third-party partners like Uber, Ticketmaster, Yelp, and more. Using frontier AI technologies – including Large Language Models (LLMs), Model Context Protocol (MCP), and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) capabilities – we create agentic experiences that connect customers with an ever-expanding selection of services through natural conversation.

As an SDE II on this team, you will design and build category-defining experiences that millions of customers interact with daily. You will work closely with product, design, and partner teams to deliver high-quality, scalable solutions that drive customer discovery, engagement, task completion, and monetization. You will contribute to category scaling frameworks – reusable components that enable partners to build sophisticated experiences with minimal effort.

We're a team that moves fast, builds with AI-native technologies, and measures success by customer impact. If you want to work at the intersection of generative AI and consumer experiences, this is the place.

Key job responsibilities
– Building high-quality, consumer-facing 3P experiences and Agentic Experts that enable customers to complete tasks through trusted partners on Alexa+.
– Designing and developing components for category scaling frameworks that enable partners to build sophisticated experiences with minimal effort.
– Collaborating closely with product, design, and partner teams to deliver experiences that drive customer discovery, engagement, and task completion.
– Understanding the business impact of your systems and showing good judgment when making technical trade-offs between speed of delivery and long-term scalability.
– Driving engineering best practices (e.g., Operational Excellence, Security, Quality) within the team.
– Contributing to the professional development of colleagues by sharing knowledge, participating in code reviews, and improving engineering practices.

A day in the life
A typical day will start with a morning huddle to catch up on what you and your team worked on the previous day. This is usually followed by some whiteboard design discussions on the problems the team is working on. The afternoon is mostly spent on project design and implementation work with a heavy dose of code reviews from and for your team along the way. We are a fun, close-knit team where everyone is ready to roll up their sleeves and help each other out, push boundaries, and have fun doing it!

About the team
Alexa 3P Experiences is a dedicated organization within Alexa Domains, purpose-built to rapidly expand the choices and selection of agentic experiences on Alexa+. Building on early launches with marquee partners, our mission is to offer customers choice and expanded selection in Alexa's agentic capabilities – positioning Alexa to lead in the next generation of ambient AI interactions. We work closely with cross-functional teams across Alexa for core platform capabilities while owning the end-to-end consumer experience and category innovation.
jobs.description_short: Alexa+ is Amazon's next-generation personal assistant powered by generative AI – more conversational, smarter, personalized, and built to get things done on any device.The Alexa 3P Experiences team is
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- 3+ years of full software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations experience
- Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Maintenance préventive et GMAO : Expérience prouvée avec les systèmes de maintenance préventive planifiée et maîtrise des plateformes GMAO
- Dépannage et diagnostic : Capacités avancées de dépannage sur équipements de manutention et systèmes d'automatisation, avec maîtrise de la lecture des plans mécaniques et électriques
- Compétences techniques : Expérience pratique avec systèmes de convoyeurs, contrôleurs moteur, variateurs de fréquence et technologies de capteurs
- Compétences opérationnelles : Connaissance des normes de santé et sécurité, fortes compétences organisationnelles et excellentes capacités de communication
- Formation et expérience : Bac Pro, BTS, BUT ou équivalent (Bac+2 minimum) en maintenance industrielle, électrotechnique ou mécanique, avec expérience en maintenance électrique et mécanique ou une expérience sur un poste similaire pour une durée d'au moins de deux ans.
jobs.description:
Technicien de Maintenance (F/H) Chaque jour, des millions de clients comptent sur Amazon pour recevoir leurs commandes à temps. Derrière cette promesse se cache un réseau d'automatisation sophistiqué où chaque seconde compte. En tant que Technicien/ne RME, vous serez au cœur de cette mécanique de précision, gardien des technologies qui permettent à nos centres de distribution de fonctionner 24h/24 et 7j/7.

Ce qui rend ce rôle unique ?
Vous travaillerez sur des équipements à la pointe de la technologie, parfois conçus en interne, dans un environnement où l'innovation rencontre l'excellence opérationnelle. Votre expertise technique aura un impact direct et mesurable : chaque intervention réussie, c'est des milliers de colis qui arrivent à destination comme prévu.
Si vous adorez les challenges techniques, que vous vous épanouissez dans des environnements dynamiques et que vous voulez voir l'impact concret de votre travail à l'échelle mondiale, ce poste est fait pour vous.


Key job responsibilities
Garantir la sécurité et la conformité
• Promouvoir les meilleures pratiques de santé et sécurité dans toutes les activités de maintenance, en assurant la conformité totale aux normes et réglementations
• Effectuer des évaluations de risques et mettre en place des protocoles de sécurité pour les travaux de maintenance
Assurer l'excellence en maintenance préventive
• Exécuter des programmes complets de maintenance préventive planifiée sur des équipements automatisés diversifiés : convoyeurs, robots, systèmes de tri et d'emballage
• Utiliser les systèmes informatisés de gestion de la maintenance (GMAO) pour suivre, programmer et documenter les activités de maintenance
• Collaborer avec les fabricants d'équipements et prestataires externes pour optimiser les procédures de maintenance
Intervenir rapidement et résoudre les problèmes complexes
• Fournir une réponse rapide aux pannes d'équipement avec communication claire aux équipes opérationnelles
• Effectuer un dépannage avancé sur les systèmes électriques, mécaniques et d'automatisation complexes
• Utiliser les outils de diagnostic et documentation technique pour identifier efficacement les causes racines
Maîtriser les technologies d'automatisation
• Maintenir et configurer des systèmes d'automatisation sophistiqués incluant les contrôleurs logiques programmables (API) et réseaux de terrain
• Entretenir et calibrer les scanners de codes-barres, systèmes d'impression-application et équipements de tri
Contribuer à l'amélioration continue
• Analyser les données de performance des équipements pour identifier les opportunités d'amélioration
• Développer et mettre en œuvre des initiatives de réduction des coûts par l'optimisation des équipements
• Partager les connaissances techniques et encadrer les techniciens juniors
Horaires de travail : Ce poste fonctionne dans un environnement 24h/24 et 7j/7 nécessitant une flexibilité avec des horaires variables, incluant nuits, week-ends et jours fériés.


A day in the life
Nos techniciens RME sont basés sur site, afin de répondre rapidement à tout problème technique affectant nos opérations. Vous effectuerez une série de travaux de maintenance préventive planifiée sur les équipements en place sur nos sites afin de vous assurer qu’ils fonctionnent et ne présentent aucun risque de sécurité. En tant que technicien RME, vous interviendrez rapidement en cas de panne, et vous mènerez l’enquête pour détecter les pannes et les solutionner aussi vite que possible. Il vous appartiendra également de faire des suggestions pour améliorer durablement les processus.
En collaboration avec des collègues expérimentés, vous effectuerez des tâches de maintenance quotidiennes telles que la peinture et la plomberie et assurerez la liaison avec les sous-traitants externes en cas de besoin si nécessaire. Vous travaillerez en équipe afin de vous assurer que nos sites bénéficient d’une assistance 24 heures sur 24, ce qui permet à Amazon de livrer ses produits à la vitesse qui fait sa réputation.

About the team
Notre équipe d’ingénieurs RME (Reliability Maintenance Engineering) veille à ce que nos équipements fonctionnent au mieux. Guidés par des responsables expérimentés, nous sommes une équipe à l’esprit analytique. Nos collaborateurs·trices possèdent à la fois des connaissances techniques poussées et un bon esprit d’équipe. Nous travaillons ensemble pour assurer l’entretien, le dépannage et la réparation des ressources de notre réseau mondial de centres d’expédition. Certains de nos employé·es occupent des postes à responsabilités et sont chargés de superviser l’équipe dans son travail sur des technologies passionnantes, dont certaines n’existent que chez Amazon.
De l’installation de systèmes d’emballage automatisés à la supervision de la maintenance générale d’un site, en passant par la réparation d’équipements de distribution essentiels, notre équipe assure le bon fonctionnement de la plupart des éléments techniques d’Amazon. Nous veillons, par exemple, à ce que les bâtiments soient conformes à la législation, afin d’assurer la sécurité de tous et le fonctionnement à plein régime de nos sites. Les possibilités d’évolution sont nombreuses, tant au sein de l’équipe RME qu’à l’échelle d’Amazon.
Tout ce que nous faisons vise à réduire les temps d’arrêt dans les principaux sites logistiques d’Amazon, afin que les clients reçoivent leurs commandes à temps. Nous travaillons souvent tôt ou tard pour effectuer les tâches de maintenance avec un minimum de perturbations, y compris en équipes de nuit. Si nous voyons qu’il existe une méthodologie plus aboutie pour faire les choses, nous avons les compétences nécessaires pour développer et introduire des processus nouveaux ou des technologies de pointe, comme Amazon Robotics ou notre robot de tri ultra sophistiqué.
jobs.description_short: Technicien de Maintenance (F/H) Chaque jour, des millions de clients comptent sur Amazon pour recevoir leurs commandes à temps. Derrière cette promesse se cache un réseau d'automatisation sophistiqué où
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Certifications électriques : Habilitations électriques avec expérience en travaux d'ordre électrique et maintenance des contrôleurs moteur/variateurs
- Expertise en automatisation : Expérience avec systèmes Siemens (API), réseaux de terrain, robotique et systèmes automatisés de stockage/récupération
- Technologies de traçabilité : Expertise en configuration et maintenance de scanners de codes-barres et systèmes d'impression-application
- Technologie de maintenance 4.0: Utilisation et/ou exploitation des différentes technologies liées à la maintenance 4.0
- Leadership technique : Expérience d'encadrement de techniciens juniors avec compétences en rédaction technique pour la documentation
- Gestion de projets : Expérience en gestion de projet d'installations d'équipements et capacités multilingues (un atout)

Amazon est un employeur engagé pour l'égalité des chances. Nous sommes convaincus qu'une main d'oeuvre diversifée est essentielle à notre réussite. Nous prenons nos décisions de recrutement en fonction de votre expérience et de vos compétences. Nous apprécions votre envie de découvrir, d'inventer, de simplifier et de construire. La protection de votre vie privée et la sécurité de vos données constituent depuis longtemps une priorité absolue pour Amazon. Veuillez consulter notre Politique de Confidentialité pour en savoir plus sur la façon dont nous collectons, utilisons et traitons les données personnelles de nos candidats.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- Experience programming with at least one software programming language
jobs.description:
Would you like to work on one of the world's largest transactional distributed systems? How about working with customers and peers from the entire range of Amazon's business on cool new features? Whether you're passionate about building highly scalable and reliable systems or a software developer who likes to solve business problems, Selling Partner Services (SPS) is the place for you. Our team is responsible for Case Management System.

We are looking for software engineers who thrive on complex problems and solve for operating complex and mission critical systems under high loads. Our systems manage case resolution systems with hundreds of millions of requests, and respond to millions of service requests. We have both frontend and backend code stack and opportunities. We are aimed to provide customizable and LLM based solution to our clients. Do you think you are up for this challenge? Or would you like to learn more and stretch your skills and career?
The successful candidate is expected to contribute to all parts of the software development and deployment lifecycle, including design, development, documentation, testing and maintenance. They must possess good verbal and written communication skills, be self-driven and deliver high quality results in a fast paced environment.
You will thrive in our collaborative environment, working alongside accomplished engineers who value teamwork and technical excellence. We're looking for experienced technical leaders.

Key job responsibilities
As a Software Development Engineer on the team you will take ownership over the software design, documentation, development, engineering approach, delivery and support of systems built natively in AWS. In this role you will collaborate with leaders, work backwards from customers, identify problems, propose innovative solutions, relentlessly raise standards, and have a positive impact on hundreds of millions of customers.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of team management experience, or a Bachelor's degree and 2+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams experience
- Experience owning the operation of a mission-critical team or product
- Experience conducting interviews and making effective hiring decisions
- High school diploma, or equivalent
- Business level English and Japanese language proficiency
jobs.description:
The Data Center Operations Manager leads a team of frontline server engineers providing critical server and network maintenance in AWS data centers. You and your team provide 24/7 frontline support, act as first responders to incidents, and support the development and implementation of improvements to maintenance procedures and practices. Data Center Managers must be effective people leaders able to hire, grow, and evaluate talent on an ongoing basis to meet business needs in a rapidly evolving technical environment. You must be able to work independently to manage your team effectively and be able to determine if and when it is necessary to escalate challenges. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to being successful.




Key job responsibilities
- Lead a team of frontline server engineers to maintain critical server and network equipment
- Hold a high bar for safety culture and security posture driving compliance across your team
- Prioritize, assign, and review repair work to meet customer SLAs
- Manage the performance of a diverse team of data center professionals across multiple locations
- Develop career paths for employees and support professional growth and skills development
- Recruit and train server engineers to maintain appropriate staffing levels and team capabilities
- Ensure effective and efficient management of day-to-day operations including repair queue management, 7/24 shift arrangement, escalations, and incident response
- Ensure all operational KPIs and metrics are being measured and met
- Inspire and guide improvement in processes, policy, and automation
- Lead investigations to identify root cause of failures, draft remediation plans, and implement long term solutions
- Maintain on-call schedules
- Be passionate about the quality and quantity of services being provided, and continuously strive to improve our Customer Experience


A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a fast paced technology company and a leader in the world of data centers. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels of data center operations. We are currently expanding our infrastructure management team and are looking for technology managers with experience in people management, strong technical understanding and the drive and vision to take AWS Data Center Operations to the next level.
jobs.description_short: The Data Center Operations Manager leads a team of frontline server engineers providing critical server and network maintenance in AWS data centers. You and your team provide 24/7 frontline support, act

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Ein Bachelorabschluss oder eine gleichwertige Qualifikation entsprechend dem Europäischen Qualifikationsrahmen (EQF)
- Zertifizierung als „Fachkraft für Arbeitssicherheit“
- Verständnis der deutschen Umwelt- und Arbeitsschutz-Gesetzgebung
- Fortgeschrittene Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse
jobs.description:
Für unseren neuen Standort in Contwig bei Zweibrücken suchen wir:

EHS Specialist / Fachkraft für Arbeitssicherheit (all genders)

Arbeitsschutz und Wohlergehen unserer Mitarbeiter:innen haben für Amazon höchste Priorität. Unsere Safety Specialists spielen dabei eine entscheidende Rolle. In dieser Funktion sorgst Du proaktiv für die Sicherheit und Gesundheit aller Mitarbeiter:innen an Deinem Standort, gibst ihnen Zuversicht und motivierst sie zu Höchstleistungen. Du kannst jeden Tag etwas für sie bewirken.

Key job responsibilities
- Überwachung der Standortleistung anhand festgelegter KPIs und Erstellung von Plänen zur Verbesserung von Prozessen
- Entwicklung von Programmen für den Arbeitsschutz und Planung von Schulungen
- Auswertung von Sicherheitskennzahlen und Trends bei Vorfällen; Unterstützung der Mitarbeiter:innen bei der Ressourcenplanung für Risikobereiche
- Durchführung von Prüfungen zur Einhaltung der Arbeitsschutzvorschriften und Umsetzung von Lösungen, wenn Risiken erkannt werden

A day in the life


Du arbeitest vor Ort an unserem Logistikstandort in Contwig. Dort nimmst Du die Prozesse genau unter die Lupe. Du überwachst die Einhaltung der Arbeitsschutzvorschriften und erkennst mögliche Probleme und Chancen für Verbesserungen. Du arbeitest mit Mitarbeiter:innen im gesamten Geschäftsbereich zusammen und lernst ihre Risiken kennen. Du sammelst Daten, um smartere und sicherere Arbeitsweisen umzusetzen. Außerdem entwickelst Du Schulungen und andere Programme, in denen Mitarbeiter:innen neue Sicherheitsprozesse erlernen.

About the team
Wenn Du ein Teil unseres Teams wirst, kannst Du folgendes erwarten:

- Einen unbefristeten Vertrag direkt mit Amazon.
- Ein attraktives Vergütungspaket mit beschränkten Mitarbeiteraktien (Restricted Stock Units durch die Amazon.com, Inc.).
- Mitarbeiterrabatte auf amazon.de sowie Amazon Extras (z.B. Rabatte in Reisen, Elektronik, etc.).
- Pendlerleistungen, z.B. Kostenübernahme für das Deutschlandticket.
- Leistungen wie z.B. Unterstützung bei der betrieblichen Altersvorsorge und eine kostenlose - Lebens- und Erwerbsunfähigkeitsversicherung.
- Mitarbeiter werben Mitarbeiter (Prämienzahlung)
- Ein innovatives Umfeld, in dem Sicherheit oberste Priorität hat und deine Stimme geschätzt wird.
- Flache Organisationsstruktur sowie die Möglichkeit, mit den klügsten Köpfen aus verschiedenen Branchen zusammenzuarbeiten.
- Zahlreiche Karrieremöglichkeiten dank unseres umfangreichen Netzwerks von Standorten in Deutschland und weltweit.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience managing team
- Experience in process improvements
jobs.description:
You should be able to motivate a team of individuals to meet targets and productivity expectations in line with company goals. You should be prepared to take responsibility for tracking the attendance and work flow of that team and work alongside the Shift Manager to highlight and rectify issues as they arise.

Key job responsibilities
• Help support daily management of department duties.
• Assign work to associates as required/needed to support workflow and daily production KPIs/goals.
• Conduct standup briefs, deliver associate feedback, conduct floor audits and vehicle inspections.
• Support in training and up-skilling new associates.
• Support with tracking, trending and reporting of departmental metrics by associates.
• Support in analyzing and understanding the reasons behind success and failure in meeting departmental delivery and FC KPIs/goals
• Tracking, reporting, maintenance of hours and schedules.
• Review and update Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documents as required.
• Improve flow of product through the station.
• Identify and address safety hazards within and participate in safety initiatives.
• Fully understand workflow, daily production goals, and number of Associates present each day.

A day in the life
• Lead by example.
• Role model for safe work behaviour’s
• Excellent communication skills (written & verbal). • Meets or exceeds departmental production KPIs/goals.
• Demonstrated problem solving & analytical skills. • Willingness to train and learn new skills. • Coach and mentor others to succeed.
• Able to handle long and short term multiple tasks, whilst working in a high stress, fast paced environment.
• Able to work weekends and provide overtime support (AM & PM).
• Must be willing to work flexible schedules / shift including night shift.
jobs.description_short: You should be able to motivate a team of individuals to meet targets and productivity expectations in line with company goals. You should be prepared to take responsibility for tracking the attendance

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree, with an anticipated graduation date after the completion of your internship.
- Fluent written and verbal communication in English (Level - B2 or higher).
- Fluent written and verbal communication in German (Level - B2 or higher).
- Available to commence an internship during 2026.
- You should then be available for 6 months, full-time (40h/week).
jobs.description:
Launch your career in the corporate world by helping businesses grow through Amazon Business. Amazon Business provides organizations of all sizes with access to a vast selection of products, competitive pricing, and convenient purchasing solutions designed specifically for business customers.

As an Account Representative Intern, you will support the growth of Amazon Business by building relationships with business customers, understanding their procurement needs, and helping them get the most value from Amazon Business tools and services.
How often can you say that your work changes the way businesses buy? At Amazon, you’ll say it often. Join us and help shape the future of B2B e-commerce.


Key job responsibilities
• Build and maintain strong relationships with business customers, acting as a trusted point of contact
• Provide tailored guidance on Amazon Business features, tools, and purchasing solutions to help customers optimize their procurement processes
• Collaborate with internal teams (Customer Support, Product, Operations, and Marketing) to resolve customer issues and improve the customer experience
• Contribute to internal projects and initiatives aimed at improving customer adoption and engagement with Amazon Business solutions


A day in the life
Your paid 6-month internship includes:

In-person collaboration
We believe that being together helps us invent, collaborate, and stay connected to our culture while delivering the best outcomes for customers and businesses.

Mentorship
We care about your career aspirations and aim to assign projects aligned with your interests. You’ll have access to a wide range of virtual training on topics such as project management, personal branding, and communication skills.

Get involved
You can join one of Amazon’s Affinity Groups and support initiatives related to sustainability, diversity, LGBTQ+, disabilities, and community engagement.

Ownership of your career
At Amazon, we encourage ownership and initiative. A successful internship may lead to a full-time graduate role after completing your studies.


About the team
The Amazon Business team at Amazon is focused on transforming how businesses purchase supplies and equipment by providing a seamless B2B shopping experience.
As an Account Representative Intern, you will work closely with experienced account managers and business development teams who support and grow Amazon Business customers across Europe.


Every day will be filled with developing new skills and achieving personal growth. Interested?
• Applying takes less than 10 minutes: just upload your CV and complete a questionnaire.
• Selected candidates will be invited to an online assessment. If successful, you will attend two rounds of virtual interviews (behavioral and case study focused on Leadership Principles), which take place on the same day.
• The entire recruitment process typically takes 3 to 4 weeks but may vary by role and location.

Base pay for this position starts from EUR 1600 gross per month. The salary depends on the skills and requirements. Dependent on the position offered, company benefits apply subject to further terms, including annual membership in medical clinic, life insurance, retirement pension, meal voucher card, career development.

EMEA AUTA Teams

jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services Infrastructure Services team provides comprehensive technical support throughout the design, planning, delivery, and operation of Amazon Web Services infrastructure. We support data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure the customers of Amazon Web Services have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations technical support managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across Amazon and our operating partners to help Amazon Web Services deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for the customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Global Network Delivery organization is looking to hire a highly motivated, best-in-class Network Project Lead to join our growing team within AWS Data Center Delivery. The candidate will work with minimum supervision in a dynamic environment to drive the stability and sustainability of our next-generation networks and to develop innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand.

The position responsibilities will include scaling support of several data center locations and be responsible for day to day assistance with capacity management. The Network Project Lead will work closely with both internal stakeholders and external vendors to facilitate smooth project execution as directed by Technical Program Managers, Network Deployment Planning team, infrastructure supply chain & procurement and network engineering team.

The ideal candidate will possess a technology background that enables them to understand the complexities of the sophisticated network architecture of Amazon data centers. To be a qualified candidate you must be highly autonomous, detail oriented, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills and have experience in managing network deployment projects. They will be competent in networking principles, data center operations, cable infrastructure and fiber solutions.

This position will provide the individual with high levels of autonomous work with minimal supervision to drive the stability and sustainability of our next-generation networks and to assist in the development of innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, ambiguous, collaborative environment is essential for success.

In addition, this role may require international travels to support other cluster’s projects.


Key job responsibilities
1. Strategic Planning & Design Management
- Contribute in developing comprehensive 6-8 months network implementation roadmaps aligned with AWS DC expansion targets
- Create and optimize network position layouts and rack placement strategies for data centre floor space utilization
- Review and contribute to High-Level Design (HLD) documentation
- Coordinate with engineering teams to validate technical requirements and design specifications
- Develop capacity planning models to ensure infrastructure scalability
- Create standardized planning templates and methodologies for network implementations

2. Project & Resource Management
- Lead end-to-end project planning for multiple concurrent network implementation initiatives
- Develop and maintain detailed project schedules, milestones, and dependencies
- Manage project scope, and quality parameters
- Conduct risk assessments and develop mitigation strategies
- Improvise and optimize planning by incorporating lessons learnt of latest deployed projects, iteratively.
- Coordinate across cross-functional teams to ensure alignment on project deliverables
- Track and report weekly project progress and/or otherwise informed to key stakeholders

3. Supply Chain & Inventory Planning
- Develop project inventory forecasts and requirements
- Optimize developed counts by utilizing prior surplus spares.
- Coordinate with procurement teams for strategic resource acquisition
- Manage vendor relationships and delivery schedules
- Establish and stick to procurement timelines aligned with project schedules

4. Program Governance & Process Excellence
- Establish and maintain project management frameworks and best practices
- Develop sustainable and repeatable planning processes
- Create and maintain project documentation and reporting standards
- Drive continuous improvement initiatives in planning methodologies
- Build and maintain stakeholder communication channels
- Coordinate with regional teams for standardized planning approaches and optimized lesson learnt based iterative optimized planning.

About the team
About Amazon Web Services
Diverse Experiences
Amazon Web Services values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why Amazon Web Services?
Amazon Web Services is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. Amazon Web Services pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust Amazon Web Services’ robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Amazon Web Services values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is looking for a Data Center Engineering Operations Engineer to join our expanding Data Centers (DC) Cloud regions. Data Center Engineering Operations Engineer is a hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting and operation responder.

As an Engineering Operations Engineer, you will operate and maintain AWS DCs’ world-class mechanical and electrical infrastructure following a process-oriented approach. In addition, you will oversee facility operations and ensure the implementation of safety procedures.

If you like to apply your electrical and mechanical skills to solve problems while striving for operational excellence, we would like to meet you. Your work will help maintain availability for customers and ensure the upkeep and operation of the technical infrastructure in the DC.

Key job responsibilities
SAFETY

- Follow and maintain the highest safety standards and diligently encourage a world-class safety culture. As a facility owner team, ensure remediation of safety risks/issues in conjunction with other teams. Own the safety initiatives and projects to foster strong safety culture.

ENGINEERING & FACILITY OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE
- You will be supporting electrical and mechanical equipment, including Uninterruptable Power Supplies (UPS’s), Switchgear, Circuit Breakers, Automatic Transfer Switches (ATS's), Diesel Generators, Chillers, Heating Ventilation Air Conditioning (HVAC), Exhaust Fans, Variable Frequency Drives (VFD's), and Transformers.
- Operate and maintain mechanical and electrical, mechanical, emergency generator, HVAC and fire/life safety equipment within the data center
- Troubleshoot facility and rack-level events within internal SLAs
- Perform root cause analysis of equipment failures
- Drive innovation while reducing operational costs in the facilities

DAY IN THE LIFE
- Work rotational shifts of 4 days of 12 hours shifts (2 days followed by 2 nights), and 4 days off (including night, weekends and holidays) / Respond to on-call rotation reaction to off-duty emergency calls
- Take daily operational readings of all mechanical and electrical equipment through routine rounds/log-taking (temperatures, voltages, currents, etc.)
- Supervise contractors who perform servicing or preventive maintenance
- Perform the following tasks, with or without reasonable accommodation:
- Occasionally Move objects/material (weight limits in accordance with regulations)
- Work in outdoor weather conditions.
- Work in noisy environments
- Work at heights and from ladders

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
